ORDER ON CROSS-COMPLAINANTS VILLA DEI FIORI CONDOMINIUM, L.P. OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE SUBMITTED BY GATEWAY BUSINESS BANK IN SUPPORT OF ITS MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE ADJUDICTION

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF GATEWAY BUSINESS BANK, MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED AND AMENDS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION GRANTED IN PART, AND DENIED IN PART. SUMMARY ADJUDICATION GRANTED AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT (PROMISSORY NOTE). CROSS-DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN AND CROSS-COMPLAINANTS FAIL TO RAISE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. THE LOAN AGREEMENT PROVIDES THAT THE LENDER HAS NO OBLIGATION TO MAKE ADVANCES, IF THE BORROWER IS IN DEFAULT (UMF #17), AND CROSS-COMPLAINANT WAS IN DEFAULT (UMF#3). NO NOTICE OF DEFAULT IS REQUIRED. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION GRANTED AS TO SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT (MODIFICATION). CROSS-DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN AND CROSS-COMPLAINANTS FAIL TO RAISE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. NO EVIDENCE THAT ANY PURPORTED ORAL MODIFICATION WAS MEMORIALIZED IN WRITING. MOTION DENIED AS TO REMAINING CAUSES OF ACTION. A CONCLUSORY STATEMENT OF THE LAW DOES NOT SUFFICE TO SHOW THAT CROSS-COMPLAINANTS' EVIDENCE IS FACTUALLY DEVOID. THE COURT ALSO CITES SECREST, AS A REASON FOR RULING. THE COURT OVERRULES ALL EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS. (DEFS' OBJECT. TO EVID. FILED, AND COPIES GIVEN TO COUNSEL AT TODAY'S HEARING.) (PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL WILL PREPARE ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF/CROSS DEFENDANT GATEWAY BUSINESS BANK, DEMURRER TO THIRD AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED AND AMENDED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER SUSTAINED, TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. CROSS-COMPLAINANTS TO PLEAD SPECIFIC TERMS OF CONTRACT, INCLUDING GATEWAY'S RELATIONSHIP TO CROSS-COMPLAINANTS, GATEWAY'S DUTY TO CROSS-COMPLAINANTS, AND BREACH UNDER THE CONTRACT. IF THE SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION IS BASED UPON THE TRUSTEE'S DEED UPON SALE OR ANY AGREEMENT, THE DOCUMENT SHOULD BE ATTACHED TO THE CROSS-COMPLAINT OR ITS ESSENTIAL TERMS PLEADED IN THE CROSS-COMPLAINT. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-13-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF/CROSS DEFENDANT GATEWAY BUSINESS BANK, DEMURRER TO THIRD AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted and amended its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer sustained, ten days leave to amend. Cross-complainants to plead specific terms of contract, including Gateway's relationship to cross-complainants, Gateway's duty to cross-complainants, and breach under the contract. If the seventh cause of action is based upon the Trustee's Deed Upon Sale or any agreement, the document should be attached to the cross-complaint or its essential terms pleaded in the cross-complaint. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

MEDIATION ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-29-2009 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jun-09-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF GATEWAY BUSINESS BANK, MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Appearances: Eric J. Rans, Esq. of Michelman & Robinson LLP, (714) 557-7990, appearing for Plaintiff and moving party; Quinlan S. Tom, Esq. of McInerney & Dillon (510) 465-7100, appearing for Defendant Villa Dei Fiori Condominiums, L.P.; Innovative Realty Services, LLC; Brian S. Baniqued, individually and as Trustee, and Theotis F. Oliphant, and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted and amends its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for summary adjudication granted in part, and denied in part. Summary adjudication granted as to the first cause of action for breach of contract (promissory note). Cross-defendant shifted the burden and cross-complainants fail to raise a triable issue of fact. The loan agreement provides that the lender has no obligation to make advances, if the borrower is in default (UMF #17), and cross-complainant was in default (UMF#3). No notice of Default is required. Motion for summary adjudication granted as to second cause of action for breach of contract (modification). Cross-defendant shifted the burden and cross-complainants fail to raise a triable issue of fact. No evidence that any purported oral modification was memorialized in writing. Motion denied as to remaining causes of action. A conclusory statement of the law does not suffice to show that cross-complainants' evidence is factually devoid. The Court also cites Secrest, as a reason for ruling. The Court overrules all evidentiary objections. (Defs' object. to Evid. filed, and copies given to counsel at today's hearing.) (Plaintiff's counsel will prepare Order.) (302/CWW/JW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759
